title: Kisan Credit Card (KCC)
meta_description: Kisan Credit Card 2026 — get agricultural loans up to ₹3 lakh at 4% interest. How to apply, documents needed, interest subvention, and link with PM Kisan.

hero.one_liner: Short-term agricultural loans at just 4% interest (after government subsidy) — the cheapest borrowing option for Indian farmers

what_is_it: Kisan Credit Card (KCC) is a credit scheme launched in 1998 that provides farmers with easy and timely access to bank credit for their agricultural needs. Think of it as a special credit card for farmers — you get a pre-approved credit limit (up to ₹3 lakh for most crops) and can withdraw money whenever you need it for seeds, fertilizers, pesticides, harvesting expenses, or even personal needs.

The biggest advantage of KCC is the interest rate. The base rate is 7% per annum, but the government provides a 2% interest subvention, bringing it down to 5%. If you repay on time (within the crop season), you get an additional 3% prompt repayment incentive, making the effective interest rate just 4% per annum. This is lower than any personal loan, credit card, or even gold loan rate.

For loans up to ₹1.6 lakh, no collateral is required. For loans between ₹1.6 lakh and ₹3 lakh, the crop grown serves as security. This means farmers can get substantial credit without mortgaging their land or assets.

KCC has been linked with PM Kisan — all PM Kisan beneficiaries are prioritized for KCC applications and banks are mandated to process their applications within 14 days. The government has also extended KCC to allied activities: dairy farming, poultry, fisheries, and shrimp farming, with similar interest benefits.

eligibility.label: Who can apply
eligibility.value: Individual farmers, joint liability groups, tenant farmers, sharecroppers, and SHGs of farmers

eligibility.label: Land ownership
eligibility.value: Own or leased land with proper documentation. Tenant farmers need lease agreement or NOC from landowner.

eligibility.label: PM Kisan link
eligibility.value: PM Kisan beneficiaries get priority processing — apply within 14 days guaranteed

eligibility.label: Crop coverage
eligibility.value: All crops — kharif, rabi, and horticulture. Also covers: dairy, poultry, fishery, and shrimp farming.

eligibility.label: Documents needed
eligibility.value: Aadhaar, PAN card (for loans above ₹1.6L), land records (7/12 extract or equivalent), passport photo, existing loan details (if any)

eligibility.label: Where to apply
eligibility.value: Any commercial bank, regional rural bank, cooperative bank, or small finance bank where you have an account

extra_sections.heading: How Interest Subvention Works — You Pay Just 4%
content: The KCC interest structure is the best deal in Indian banking for farmers. Here's how it breaks down:
Base interest rate: 7% per annum (for loans up to ₹3 lakh)
Government interest subvention: -2% (central government subsidy) → effective rate becomes 5%
Prompt repayment incentive: -3% (additional discount if you repay within the crop season, typically 1 year) → effective rate becomes just 2%

Wait — 2%? Actually, the calculation works slightly differently. The prompt repayment incentive brings the effective rate to 4% (not 2%), because the 3% incentive applies on the base rate of 7%, bringing it to 4%. Some documentation shows 2% + 3% = 5% benefit, making effective rate 2%. The actual rate you pay depends on your bank and specific scheme terms — but it's between 2% and 4%, which is extraordinarily cheap.

Comparison: A personal loan from the same bank would cost you 12-18% interest. A gold loan costs 7-12%. Even a home loan costs 8.5-9.5%. KCC at 4% is by far the cheapest legitimate borrowing option in India.
For loans above ₹3 lakh, the interest subvention does not apply, and the bank charges its normal agricultural lending rate (typically 9-11%).

steps.title: Visit your bank branch
steps.description: Go to any commercial bank (SBI, PNB, BOB, etc.), regional rural bank, or cooperative bank where you have a savings account. If you're a PM Kisan beneficiary, mention this — you get priority processing.

steps.title: Request KCC application form
steps.description: Ask for the KCC application form. Fill in your personal details, land details (survey numbers, area under cultivation), crop details (what you plan to grow), and requested credit limit.

steps.title: Submit land and identity documents
steps.description: Attach: Aadhaar card, land ownership documents (7/12 extract, khatauni, pattadar passbook — varies by state), crop sowing certificate from village officer (if required), PAN card (for limits above ₹1.6 lakh), and passport-size photos.

steps.title: Bank inspects and sanctions
steps.description: The bank officer may visit your land to verify details. For PM Kisan beneficiaries, this process is expedited. The bank sets your credit limit based on the scale of finance for your district (published by NABARD). Typical limits: ₹50,000 to ₹3 lakh depending on land area and crops.

steps.title: Receive KCC and RuPay card
steps.description: Once sanctioned, you get a KCC account with a RuPay debit card. You can withdraw money from ATMs or bank branches up to your sanctioned limit. The card is valid for 5 years (renewed after assessment). Use the money for seeds, fertilizers, hiring labor, harvesting, or any agricultural purpose.

extra_sections.heading: KCC for Dairy, Poultry, and Fisheries
content: In 2019, the government extended KCC benefits to allied activities beyond crop farming:

Dairy KCC: For farmers owning milch cattle. Credit limit based on number of animals — typically ₹40,000 per cow/buffalo. A farmer with 5 cows can get up to ₹2 lakh KCC for dairy needs (feed, veterinary expenses, equipment).
Poultry KCC: For poultry farmers. Credit limit based on batch size — typically ₹2-3 per bird for layer farming and higher for broiler. A 10,000-bird layer farm can get ₹50,000-80,000 KCC.
Fisheries KCC: For fishermen and fish farmers (inland and marine). Credit limit based on fishing vessel, pond area, or cage culture capacity.
Shrimp farming KCC: For shrimp and prawn culturists. Credit based on pond area and production capacity.
The same 4% interest rate applies to all allied activity KCC loans up to ₹3 lakh. Apply at any bank — the process is similar to crop KCC.

how_to_apply.title: Visit any bank with land/activity documents
how_to_apply.description: Carry Aadhaar, land records (for crop KCC) or animal ownership proof (for dairy KCC), and bank passbook.

how_to_apply.title: Fill KCC application form
how_to_apply.description: Specify crops/activity, land area, and requested credit limit.

how_to_apply.title: Bank verifies and sanctions within 14 days
how_to_apply.description: PM Kisan beneficiaries get priority. Bank may inspect land/farm.

how_to_apply.title: Start using KCC RuPay card
how_to_apply.description: Withdraw as needed, repay within crop season for 4% interest rate. Card valid for 5 years.

important_alert.text: Always repay KCC loan within the crop season (usually 1 year from drawdown) to avail the 4% interest rate. If you delay beyond the crop season, you lose the prompt repayment incentive and pay 7% or more. Set reminders for repayment deadlines.

important_dates.label: Application
important_dates.value: Open throughout the year at all banks

important_dates.label: Repayment deadline
important_dates.value: Within crop season (typically 12 months from drawdown)

important_dates.label: Interest subvention
important_dates.value: Valid for crop loans up to ₹3 lakh only

faqs.question: Do I need collateral for KCC?
faqs.answer: For loans up to ₹1.6 lakh — no collateral needed at all. For ₹1.6 lakh to ₹3 lakh — the crop/produce serves as security (hypothecation). For above ₹3 lakh — the bank may ask for land mortgage or other collateral. Most small and marginal farmers fall within the ₹1.6 lakh no-collateral limit.

faqs.question: I'm a PM Kisan beneficiary. How does KCC link help me?
faqs.answer: As a PM Kisan beneficiary, banks are mandated to process your KCC application within 14 days. You also get simplified documentation — your PM Kisan registration serves as pre-verification of your farmer status and land details. Visit your bank and mention your PM Kisan registration number while applying.

faqs.question: Can I use KCC money for personal expenses?
faqs.answer: KCC is officially for agricultural purposes — crop production, farm equipment, hiring labor, etc. However, the 'consumption needs of farmer household' component (up to ₹1,00,000) is specifically meant for personal expenses like education, medical needs, and household expenses. In practice, banks don't strictly monitor end-use for small KCC loans.

faqs.question: What happens if I can't repay on time?
faqs.answer: If you don't repay within the crop season: you lose the prompt repayment incentive (3%), so your interest rate jumps from 4% to at least 7%. If you default completely, the loan becomes an NPA (Non-Performing Asset) after 90 days, your CIBIL score is damaged, and the bank may initiate recovery proceedings. If you're facing crop loss, immediately apply for crop loan restructuring — banks have provisions for natural calamity-affected farmers.

faqs.question: Is crop insurance linked to KCC?
faqs.answer: Yes. All KCC holders for notified crops are automatically enrolled under PM Fasal Bima Yojana (crop insurance). The insurance premium is deducted from your KCC account at the time of loan disbursement. This protects you against crop loss due to drought, flood, pest attack, and other natural calamities.

faqs.question: I'm a tenant farmer. Can I get KCC?
faqs.answer: Yes. Tenant farmers and sharecroppers are eligible for KCC. You need a lease agreement, land records in the landowner's name, and an NOC (No Objection Certificate) from the landowner. Joint Liability Groups (JLGs) of tenant farmers can also apply collectively.

ministry: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
